findings.input: Stripe-Signature: t=1717,v1=  (truncated v1 segment, no '=' value)
findings.detail: Splitting the header on ',' then on '=' assumes a value follows every key; a truncated 'v1=' with no bytes after drives an index past the slice bound. ASan flags a 1-byte OOB read; in production this is a panic -> 500, remotely triggerable on the request path.
findings.remediation: Bounds-check each split segment; reject malformed headers before indexing.
findings.blocking_note: Real bug, but availability-only and not part of an exploitable confidentiality/integrity chain — triage scores it Medium and it ships as tracked debt with an SLA, NOT a blocker.
findings.source: implementing-fuzz-testing-in-cicd-with-aflplusplus (CI crash gate + crash triage / dedup by stack trace)
